data.title: AAR inks exclusive defense deal with AmSafe Bridport
data.description:
• Aviation services firm AAR (NYSE:AIR) on Thursday said that it has signed a multi-year exclusive defensive distribution agreement with AmSafe Bridport, a TransDigm company.
  • AAR to be exclusive distributor for KC-46 and C-40 platform products in global defense and military aftermarket.
  • Agreement covers direct and indirect sales to U.S. Defense Logistics Agency, U.S. Armed Services, and foreign militaries including Japan.
  • Enhances AAR’s government contract support and customer service via its Government Programs business.
  • Expands AAR’s defense distribution portfolio to include cargo handling products.
  • AIR -0.77% after hours to $75.22
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dell in charts: Revenue from Infrastructure Solutions Group rises 44% to record $16.8B
IREN stock climbs after Q4 adjusted EBITDA tops consensus
data.description:
IREN Limited (NASDAQ:IREN) stock jumped 8.1% in Thursday after-hours trading after the bitcoin (BTC-USD) miner and data center operator posted fiscal Q4 adjusted EBITDA that topped the average analyst estimate.

Meanwhile, the company said it's approaching $1.25B total annualized revenue, with scope for further growth ahead. It estimates more than $1B annualized revenue from bitcoin mining under current mining economics, and $200M-$250M annualized revenue from AI Clouid at 10.9K Nvidia GPUs by December 2025.

"FY25 was a breakout year financially and operationally, with record results that included strong net income and more than 10x EBITDA growth," said Co-Founder and CEO Daniel Roberts.

Q4 adjusted EBITDA of $121.9M, beating the consensus estimate of $111.2M, jumped from $82.9M in Q3 and $2.5M in Q1. Adjusted EBITDA included an unrealized gain on financial instruments of $147.7M in Q4 2025, and Q3 included an unrealized loss on financial instruments of $37.9M.

Q4 revenue of $187.3M, missing the consensus of $187.5M) increased from $144.8M in Q3 and $52.8M in Q1 2025.

Total operating income of -$113.8 vs. -$73.8M in the prior quarter.

Conference  call at 5:00 PM ET.

data.title: U.S. grants FAST-41 coverage to enCore Energy's Dewey-Burdock ISR uranium project
data.description:
enCore Energy (NASDAQ:EU) closed +3.1% on Thursday after the Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council announced FAST-41 coverage for the company's Dewey-Burdock in situ recovery uranium project in South Dakota.

enCore Energy (NASDAQ:EU) previously completed significant federal review for the project and applied for FAST-41 coverage to "take advantage of the predictability, transparency and accountability of the program for the remaining permitting milestones," the Permitting Council said.

FAST-41 covered projects are entitled to comprehensive permitting timetables and transparent, collaborative management of those timetables on the Federal Permitting Dashboard.

This project is the ninth mining project to receive FAST-41 coverage since President Trump took office.

data.title: Ulta gives FY25 guidance another glow-up after a strong second quarter
data.description:
Shares of Ulta (NASDAQ:ULTA) were gaining ground in postmarket trading after the beauty retailer raised its outlook for the full year, reflecting another better-than-expected quarter and progress in its “Ulta Beauty Unleashed” strategy.

“Outstanding top line performance, fueled by growth across all major categories, drove market share growth and better-than-expected profitability,” said Ulta CEO Kecia Steelman.

For the quarter ended August 2, Ulta (NASDAQ:ULTA) earned a profit of $5.78 per share, up from $5.30 a year ago and $0.74 better than expected. Additionally, the company’s gross profit margin widened 90 basis points to 39.2% versus 38.1% estimates.

Fueled by a 6.7% increase in comparable sales (vs +2.98% estimates), 3.7% gain in transactions, and 2.9% gain in average ticket amount, total sales were up 9.3% to $2.8B compared to $2.61B estimates

For the full year, although Ulta (NASDAQ:ULTA) continues to brace for near-term economic uncertainties, it raised guidance a second time and now expects sales to be within the range of $12B to $12.1B versus a prior range of $11.5B to $11.7B. The current guidance exceeds the consensus forecast of $11.72B.

Ulta (ULTA) also raised its outlook for comparable sales to up 2.5% to 3.5% from flat to up 1.5%, and operating margin to 11.9% to 12.0% from 11.7% to $11.8% previously. This compares to estimates of +1.92% and 11.9%, respectively.

The company also raised its profit outlook for the year to a range of $23.85 to $24.30 from a prior range of $22.65 to $23.20, the midpoint of which exceeds the consensus estimate of $23.73.

The results fueled a 6% rally in shares late Thursday, setting Ulta (ULTA) up for another 17-month high at Friday’s regular open.

data.title: Movano Health granted listing extension by Nasdaq
data.description:
• Movano Health (NASDAQ:MOVE) on Thursday said it received a listing extension from the Nasdaq Hearings Panel on August 27, 2025.
  • Extension requires compliance with Listing Rule 5250(c)(1) by filing Q1 and Q2 2025 10-Qs by September 30, 2025.
  • Must also comply with Listing Rule 5550(a)(2) requiring the maintenance of $1.00 per share bid price, on or before October 30, 2025.
  • Panel decision follows August 19, 2025 hearing reviewing company's compliance plan.
  • Additionally, on August 19, 2025, the company filed a Preliminary Proxy Statement for its Annual Shareholders Meeting to be held on September 26, 2025, at which the company's shareholders will have the opportunity to approve a reverse stock split proposal, the effect of which will allow the company to demonstrate compliance rule within the extension period granted by the panel.
  • MOVE +17.61% after hours to $0.7264
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: PG&E says Diablo Canyon power plant enters extended operations
data.description:
PG&E (NYSE:PCG) said Wednesday that Unit 2 of its Diablo Canyon power plant in San Luis Obispo, California, officially entered extended operations, one day after the company originally planned to shut it down.

PG&E's (NYSE:PCG) agreement with environmental and labor groups to retire Unit 1 in November 2024 and Unit 2 in August 2025 was approved by state regulators in 2018, but California lawmakers in 2022 directed the plant to continue operations after statewide rotating outages in 2020 and further demand projections in 2022; the Unit 1 reactor began extended operations in November 2024, when it originally was expected to go offline.

PG&E (PCG) said it has asked the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission to renew its Diablo Canyon license to allow the plant to operate through 2045.

Diablo Canyon generates electricity for 4M california, providing ~10% of the state's electricity and nearly 20% of its clean, carbon-free energy.

data.title: Ambarella soars as Q2 results, guidance top estimates
data.description:
Ambarella (NASDAQ:AMBA) shares soared 20% in extended-trading on Thursday after the semiconductor company posted second-quarter results and guidance that topped estimates by a wide margin. 

For the period ending July 31, the Santa Clara, Calif.-based Ambarella said it earned an adjusted $0.15 per share as revenue jumped 49.9% year-over-year to $95.5M. Analysts were expecting adjusted earnings of $0.05 per share and $90M in sales.

“We are experiencing strong secular growth in the edge AI market, with an expanding breadth of applications driving unit demand, often with our higher priced 5nm AI SoCs,” Ambarella CEO and President Fermi Wang said in a statement. “For example, a variety of portable AI video applications have reached production status, and by the end of fiscal 2026 we expect to realize production shipments from our first robotic aerial drone win. Furthermore, our first edge infrastructure win is expected to enter production in fiscal 2027.” 

Looking to the next quarter, Ambarella said it expects revenue to be between $100M and $108M, with adjusted gross margins between 60% and 61.5%. Analysts were expecting $91.2M in sales. 

The company also now expects full-year revenue to grow between 31% and 35% (or between $373.17M and $384.6M), well above the $349.77M estimate.

data.title: Affirm Holdings issues strong FY2026 guidance after delivering Q4 earnings, revenue beat
data.description:
Affirm Holdings (NASDAQ:AFRM) stock climbed 9.6% in Thursday after-hours trading after the company's gross merchandise volume logged strong gains in fiscal Q4, helping to deliver an earnings and revenue beat. With that momentum, it issued solid guidance for FY2026 and Q1.

The buy now, pay later lending platform expects FY2026 gross merchandise volume of more than $46B (Visible Alpha consensus $45.7B). Its outlook for full-year revenue is ~8.4% of GMV, or ~$3.86B ($3.89B consensus). Adjusted operating margin is seen at more than 26.1% vs. 24.1% in FY 2025.

Affirm (NASDAQ:AFRM) expects Q1 revenue of $855M-$885M vs. $858.4M consensus; and GMV of $10.1B-$10.4 vs. Visible Alpha estimate of $9.83B.  Adjusted operating margin is expected to be 23%-25% vs. 27.0% in Q4 2025.

Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.20, topping the average analyst estimate of $0.11, climbed from $0.01 in Q3 and -$0.14 in last year’s Q4.

Total net revenue of $876.4M for the quarter ended June 30, 2025, beating the $837.1M consensus, increased from $783.1M in the prior quarter and $659.2M in the year-ago period.

GMV volume climbed to $10.4B from $8.6B in Q3 and $7.2B in last year’s Q4, exceeding the $9.64B Visible Alpha consensus. The Y/Y growth was driven by the strength with its largest merchant partners, 0% APR monthly installment loans, and the direct-to-consumer business, including Affirm Card. 

Q4 Affirm (AFRM) Card attach rate reached 10%. Card GMV more than doubled to $1.2B, and active cardholders jumped surged 97% to 2.3M, the company said.

The number of active consumers, excluding the discontinued Returnly business, rose 24% Y/Y to 23.0M at June 30, 2025. 

Adjusted operating margin was 27.0% vs. 22.2% in the prior quarter and 22.7% a year ago.

Funding capacity grew to $26.1B at June, 30, 025, from $23.3B at March 31.

Conference call at 5:00 PM ET.

Earlier, Affirm GAAP EPS of $0.20 beats by $0.09, revenue of $876.42M beats by $39.37M

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Dell guides FY25 above estimates after FQ2 double beat
